preventive-dentistry.title: Stop Problems Before They Start — Preventive Care That Saves Smiles & Money
preventive-dentistry.subheading: The dentistry you don't notice is the dentistry that saves you the most. Routine cleanings, exams and fluoride catch tiny issues before they become $3,000 surprises.

paragraphs: Every six-month visit is far more than scraping plaque. Our hygienists screen for gum disease, oral cancer, jaw issues, enamel wear, and early decay — issues that quietly cost patients tens of thousands of dollars when caught late.
We pair clinical excellence with comfort: ultrasonic scalers, fluoride foam, and a relaxed pace that respects your time and your nerves.
Stop Decay Early Catch tiny cavities while they're still painless and inexpensive to fix.
Save Your Heart Gum disease links directly to heart disease. Clean gums protect your whole body.
Save Thousands Long-Term Patients on a 6-month plan spend 60% less on dentistry over their lifetime.
Brighter, Whiter Smile Professional cleaning removes coffee, wine and tobacco stains brushing can't touch.
We review your medical history, medications, and any concerns since your last visit.
Visual exam, gum-pocket measurements, oral cancer screening and digital X-rays as needed.
Ultrasonic scaling, polish, fluoride application — gentle and thorough.
Walk out with a clear written plan: next visit, home care tips, and any flagged work.
Whether you're a brand-new patient, a parent booking the whole family, or a senior managing complex needs — prevention is the smartest dental investment you'll ever make.
bullets: Children (we recommend first visit by age 1)
Seniors managing dry mouth or root sensitivity
Patients with braces, implants, or crowns
Anyone who's avoided the dentist for a while

paragraphs: Twice a year for most adults. Patients with gum disease, smokers, or those with crowns/implants may need 3–4 visits per year. We'll recommend a custom interval after your first exam.
Most plans cover 2 cleanings per year at 80–100%. We bill insurance directly and quote any out-of-pocket amount up-front — no surprises.
Rarely. We use ultrasonic scalers (much gentler than scraping) and can apply topical numbing for sensitive areas. Tell us — we'll adjust.
You're far from alone — we welcome you back with zero judgment. Expect a more thorough first cleaning and a gentle plan to get you healthy again.
Yes, professionally applied topical fluoride is one of the most-studied treatments in medicine and dramatically reduces childhood cavities.
Direct insurance billing — most patients pay $0 out-of-pocket.

faqs.question: How often should I really come in?
faqs.answer: Twice a year for most adults. Patients with gum disease, smokers, or those with crowns/implants may need 3–4 visits per year. We'll recommend a custom interval after your first exam.

faqs.question: Will my insurance cover cleanings?
faqs.answer: Most plans cover 2 cleanings per year at 80–100%. We bill insurance directly and quote any out-of-pocket amount up-front — no surprises.

faqs.question: Does cleaning hurt if I have sensitive teeth?
faqs.answer: Rarely. We use ultrasonic scalers (much gentler than scraping) and can apply topical numbing for sensitive areas. Tell us — we'll adjust.

faqs.question: What if I haven't been to a dentist in years?
faqs.answer: You're far from alone — we welcome you back with zero judgment. Expect a more thorough first cleaning and a gentle plan to get you healthy again.

faqs.question: Is fluoride safe for children?
faqs.answer: Yes, professionally applied topical fluoride is one of the most-studied treatments in medicine and dramatically reduces childhood cavities.

preventive-dentistry.cta: Book your six-month cleaning today Same-week appointments available, evenings and weekends included. New patients welcome — and yes, we'll bill your insurance d

dental-implants.title: Permanent Teeth That Look, Feel & Function Like Your Own
dental-implants.subheading: Replace one tooth or a full arch with implants that bite, chew and smile like nature intended. All-inclusive pricing starts at $3,000 — and we mean all-inclusive.

paragraphs: A dental implant is a titanium root that fuses with your jawbone — exactly the way a natural tooth root does. We then attach a custom porcelain crown on top, indistinguishable from the teeth around it.
Unlike bridges (which damage neighboring teeth) or removable dentures (which slip and shrink your jaw), implants stand alone, preserve bone, and last decades.
bullets: Bite force equal to natural teeth

paragraphs: Permanent Solution Properly placed implants typically last 25+ years — often a lifetime.
Preserve Your Jaw Implants stimulate bone like natural roots — preventing the sunken-face look of dentures.
Eat What You Love Steak, apples, corn on the cob — no foods are off-limits with implants.
All-Inclusive Pricing $3,000 covers consult, surgery, healing cap, abutment and crown — no hidden fees.
Cone-beam CT scan to map bone density, nerves and ideal placement angle.
Comfortable in-office surgery under local anaesthetic or IV sedation. Most patients return to work next day.
Over 3–6 months, your bone fuses with the titanium post. You leave with a temporary tooth in the meantime.
Custom porcelain crown placed and adjusted for a perfect bite. You walk out with a permanent new tooth.
Almost any healthy adult is a candidate. We've successfully placed implants in patients from 22 to 92 — including those who were told 'no' elsewhere.
bullets: Missing a single tooth or multiple teeth
Want to avoid grinding down healthy teeth for a bridge
Have adequate bone (or interested in grafting options)
Non-smokers (or willing to quit during healing)
Generally healthy — diabetes is fine when well-managed

paragraphs: Less than most patients expect. The procedure is done under local anaesthetic (or IV sedation if you prefer). Most patients describe post-op discomfort as 'a sore jaw for 2–3 days' — managed easily with ibuprofen.
From initial scan to final crown: typically 4–6 months. The bulk is bone-healing time, which you go about your normal life through (with a temporary tooth in place).
Some plans cover up to 50% of the crown portion. We submit pre-determination so you know exactly what's covered before any treatment begins.
Bone grafting is a routine same-day procedure. We've successfully implanted patients who were told elsewhere they couldn't have implants.
Yes — full-arch implants (All-on-4 or All-on-6) replace an entire upper or lower with just 4–6 implants. We'll quote it during your free consult.
All-Inclusive Single Implant
Includes consult, surgery, abutment and final crown. Financing available.
30-minute consult, complimentary 3D scan, written quote — yours to take home. Zero pressure, zero commitment.

faqs.question: Is implant surgery painful?
faqs.answer: Less than most patients expect. The procedure is done under local anaesthetic (or IV sedation if you prefer). Most patients describe post-op discomfort as 'a sore jaw for 2–3 days' — managed easily with ibuprofen.

faqs.question: How long does the whole process take?
faqs.answer: From initial scan to final crown: typically 4–6 months. The bulk is bone-healing time, which you go about your normal life through (with a temporary tooth in place).

faqs.question: Will insurance cover any of it?
faqs.answer: Some plans cover up to 50% of the crown portion. We submit pre-determination so you know exactly what's covered before any treatment begins.

faqs.question: What if I don't have enough bone?
faqs.answer: Bone grafting is a routine same-day procedure. We've successfully implanted patients who were told elsewhere they couldn't have implants.

faqs.question: Can I get all my teeth replaced?
faqs.answer: Yes — full-arch implants (All-on-4 or All-on-6) replace an entire upper or lower with just 4–6 implants. We'll quote it during your free consult.

dental-crowns-bridges.title: Restore Damaged Teeth. Regain a Confident Bite.
dental-crowns-bridges.subheading: Whether a tooth is cracked, root-canalled, or missing entirely — modern crowns and bridges look identical to your natural teeth and last 15+ years.

paragraphs: A crown is a custom-fit porcelain cap that covers a damaged tooth — protecting it from further breakage and restoring full chewing function. A bridge uses crowns on adjacent teeth to 'bridge' a gap left by a missing tooth.
Both are made from premium tooth-coloured porcelain, hand-tinted to match your existing teeth so precisely that even you won't be able to tell.
Prevents Further Damage A crown holds together a cracked tooth — saving it from extraction down the line.
Perfect Aesthetic Match Hand-tinted porcelain matches your surrounding teeth in colour, translucency and shape.
Restores Full Function Bite into anything — apples, steak, almonds — with the same force as a natural tooth.
Long-Lasting A well-cared-for porcelain crown lasts 15–25 years. Bridges typically last 10–15.
We gently shape the tooth, take a digital impression (no goopy mould) and fit a comfortable temporary crown.
Our master ceramist hand-crafts your crown in our local lab to match your exact tooth shade.
Two weeks later we check fit, colour and bite — adjusting until it's invisible and feels perfect.
Final cementation. You walk out chewing normally — ready for the next 15+ years of smiling.
Crowns rescue teeth that fillings can't save. Bridges replace single missing teeth without surgery — a great option when implants aren't right for you.
bullets: Cracked, broken or severely worn tooth
Just had a root canal (crown protects the tooth)
Missing a single tooth between healthy neighbours
Want a non-surgical alternative to an implant

paragraphs: Yes — our ceramist hand-tints each crown to match your natural shade, including the subtle translucency at the edge. Most patients can't pick which tooth is the crown.
Two visits, about 90 minutes total. Visit 1 (prep + temporary) is the longer one; visit 2 (final fit) is usually under 30 minutes.
After 1–2 weeks of getting used to it, no. The bite is calibrated to your exact occlusion.
Different problems. A crown saves an existing tooth; an implant replaces a missing one. If your tooth can be saved with a crown, that's usually the better choice.
Most plans cover 50% after deductible. We submit pre-determination so you'll know your exact out-of-pocket before we start.
Per Tooth-Coloured Crown
Bridges from $2,800. We bill insurance directly and offer financing.
Book a 30-minute consult — we'll look, take a quick X-ray, and quote your treatment in writing before you leave.

faqs.question: Will the crown match my other teeth?
faqs.answer: Yes — our ceramist hand-tints each crown to match your natural shade, including the subtle translucency at the edge. Most patients can't pick which tooth is the crown.

faqs.question: How long does the procedure take?
faqs.answer: Two visits, about 90 minutes total. Visit 1 (prep + temporary) is the longer one; visit 2 (final fit) is usually under 30 minutes.

faqs.question: Will it feel different than my natural tooth?
faqs.answer: After 1–2 weeks of getting used to it, no. The bite is calibrated to your exact occlusion.

faqs.question: Crown vs. implant — which is better?
faqs.answer: Different problems. A crown saves an existing tooth; an implant replaces a missing one. If your tooth can be saved with a crown, that's usually the better choice.

faqs.question: Does insurance cover crowns?
faqs.answer: Most plans cover 50% after deductible. We submit pre-determination so you'll know your exact out-of-pocket before we start.

dentures-partials.title: Comfortable, Custom Dentures That Look & Feel Like You
dentures-partials.subheading: Modern dentures are nothing like the loose, plastic-looking sets your grandparents had. Today's dentures fit snugly, look natural, and let you eat almost anything.

paragraphs: Whether you're missing all your teeth (full denture) or just a few (partial denture), we craft each set in our local lab to your exact bite, jaw shape and facial features.
Want even more stability? Implant-retained dentures snap securely onto two to four implants — eliminating slipping, glue, and the gradual jaw shrinkage of traditional dentures.
bullets: Hand-shaded to match your skin tone

paragraphs: Eat With Confidence Properly-fit dentures restore 80–90% of natural bite force — most foods are back on the menu.
Look Years Younger Dentures restore the facial support lost with missing teeth — reducing the 'sunken' look.
Affordable Restoration Less expensive than full-mouth implants and covered substantially by most insurance plans.
Implant-Upgrade Anytime Start with a traditional denture and add implant snaps later as budget allows.
We assess your gums, jaw and remaining teeth. Multiple impressions ensure a precise fit.
Try on a wax model to perfect tooth colour, shape and bite before final fabrication.
Hand-finished dentures fitted, adjusted and balanced. We tweak until they're comfortable.
Free fine-tuning visits during the adjustment period. We won't stop until they feel perfect.
If you're missing several or all your teeth and prefer a non-surgical solution, modern dentures may be your best path back to a full, confident smile.
bullets: Missing all upper or lower teeth
Open to upgrading to implant-retained later
Current dentures that are loose or 10+ years old

paragraphs: Today's dentures are stunning. We custom-tint each tooth (slight variation looks more natural than perfectly uniform), shape the gum line to match your existing tissue, and balance the bite for a relaxed, natural smile.
2–4 weeks for most patients. We schedule follow-ups during this period to adjust pressure points and answer questions.
After the adjustment period — mostly yes. Steak and corn on the cob can be tricky with full upper dentures; implant-retained dentures solve this entirely.
Soak overnight in denture cleaner, brush gently with a denture brush each morning. We'll demonstrate at delivery.
A full denture runs $1,500–$3,500. Full-arch implants run $20,000+. Many patients start with a denture and add implant retention ($5,000) later as budget allows.
Full or Partial Denture
Implant-retained option from $6,500. Direct insurance billing available.
Free consult, written quote, and a no-pressure walk-through of all your options — including implant-supported alternatives.

faqs.question: Will they look fake?
faqs.answer: Today's dentures are stunning. We custom-tint each tooth (slight variation looks more natural than perfectly uniform), shape the gum line to match your existing tissue, and balance the bite for a relaxed, natural smile.

faqs.question: How long until I get used to them?
faqs.answer: 2–4 weeks for most patients. We schedule follow-ups during this period to adjust pressure points and answer questions.

faqs.question: Can I eat normally?
faqs.answer: After the adjustment period — mostly yes. Steak and corn on the cob can be tricky with full upper dentures; implant-retained dentures solve this entirely.

faqs.question: How do I clean them?
faqs.answer: Soak overnight in denture cleaner, brush gently with a denture brush each morning. We'll demonstrate at delivery.

faqs.question: What's the cost difference vs. implants?
faqs.answer: A full denture runs $1,500–$3,500. Full-arch implants run $20,000+. Many patients start with a denture and add implant retention ($5,000) later as budget allows.

tooth-colored-fillings.title: Cavities, Repaired Invisibly — No Metal, No Mercury, No Black Spots
tooth-colored-fillings.subheading: Modern composite fillings bond directly to your tooth, look completely natural, and contain zero mercury. You'll forget which tooth was even repaired.

paragraphs: Silver (amalgam) fillings expand and contract with temperature, slowly cracking the tooth around them. They also contain mercury and are visible whenever you laugh or yawn.
Composite resin fillings bond chemically to your tooth, expand at the same rate as enamel, and match your tooth colour perfectly. They're the safer, better-looking choice in every way.
Mercury-Free Our composite contains zero mercury — safer for you and the environment.
Invisible Repair Hand-shaded to your exact tooth colour — even your mirror won't spot it.
Preserves More Tooth Composite bonds to enamel, so less healthy tooth needs to be removed.
Same-Day Treatment Most fillings are completed in one 45–60 minute visit.
Topical gel then a gentle injection so you feel nothing during the procedure.
Carefully removes only the decayed portion — preserving as much healthy tooth as possible.
Tooth-coloured composite is placed in layers, each cured with a special light.
We sculpt and polish until it matches the natural contour and shine of your tooth.
Cavities don't heal on their own. Whether it's a new spot or replacing an aging silver filling, composite is the modern standard.
bullets: Sensitivity to hot, cold or sweet foods
Visible dark spot or hole in a tooth
X-ray flagged a cavity at last cleaning

paragraphs: Only if they're failing — cracked, leaking, or causing sensitivity. We don't recommend replacing healthy silver fillings just for aesthetics, but we're happy to discuss your options.
No. We numb thoroughly and check before starting. Most patients say they barely felt anything.
10–15 years for most patients, sometimes longer. Silver fillings last slightly longer (15–25 years) but the trade-offs aren't worth it for most.
Yes — fillings are typically covered at 80% after deductible. We bill directly and quote any difference up-front.
Very early decay (only in the enamel) can sometimes be remineralized with fluoride. Once decay reaches the dentin, a filling is required.
Per Composite Filling
Most plans cover 80%. Insurance billed directly.
Most fillings done same-day. Bring us your X-rays from another office and we'll review free — no second-opinion fee.

faqs.question: Should I replace my old silver fillings?
faqs.answer: Only if they're failing — cracked, leaking, or causing sensitivity. We don't recommend replacing healthy silver fillings just for aesthetics, but we're happy to discuss your options.

faqs.question: Does the procedure hurt?
faqs.answer: No. We numb thoroughly and check before starting. Most patients say they barely felt anything.

faqs.question: How long do composite fillings last?
faqs.answer: 10–15 years for most patients, sometimes longer. Silver fillings last slightly longer (15–25 years) but the trade-offs aren't worth it for most.

faqs.question: Will my insurance cover it?
faqs.answer: Yes — fillings are typically covered at 80% after deductible. We bill directly and quote any difference up-front.

faqs.question: Can a cavity be reversed without a filling?
faqs.answer: Very early decay (only in the enamel) can sometimes be remineralized with fluoride. Once decay reaches the dentin, a filling is required.

root-canal.title: Same-Day Pain Relief. Save Your Tooth, Not Replace It.
root-canal.subheading: A root canal isn't the horror story your grandparents told. Modern technique is comfortable, often completed in one visit, and saves a tooth that would otherwise need extraction.

paragraphs: When a tooth's inner pulp gets infected — usually from deep decay or trauma — the pain can be debilitating. A root canal removes the infected tissue, disinfects the inside of the tooth, and seals it so the tooth can stay in place.
Modern rotary instruments and surgical microscopes have transformed the procedure. Most patients leave saying it was 'no worse than a filling' — and the throbbing pain is finally gone.
bullets: Most cases completed in one visit

paragraphs: Immediate Pain Relief The throbbing, sleep-stealing pain stops the moment we remove the infected pulp.
Save Your Natural Tooth Far better than extraction — keeps your bite, jaw bone and smile intact.
Prevents Spread Stops the infection from reaching your jawbone or bloodstream.
Often One Visit Most root canals (especially front teeth) are done in a single 60–90 minute appointment.
X-ray confirms the issue. We numb the area thoroughly — most patients feel nothing.
Tiny rotary files clean the canal under surgical microscope precision.
Antibacterial irrigation, then biocompatible filling material seals the canal.
Within 2–3 weeks, we place a protective crown to prevent future fracture.
Some symptoms scream 'root canal needed' — others are subtle. If any of these sound familiar, see us before the infection worsens.
bullets: Sharp pain to hot or cold (especially lingering)
Throbbing pain that wakes you at night
Swollen gum near the painful tooth

paragraphs: No — and this is the myth we love debunking. The pain you feel before a root canal is from the infection. The procedure itself, with modern anaesthetic, is no more uncomfortable than a filling. Patients are stunned.
60–90 minutes for most teeth. Molars (with multiple canals) may need two visits, about 90 minutes each.
Yes — root-canalled teeth become brittle and need a crown to prevent cracking. We typically place this within 2–3 weeks after the root canal heals.
Saving your natural tooth is almost always better. Extraction means losing the tooth permanently, then needing a bridge or implant — far more expensive and disruptive.
Yes, most plans cover 50–80% after deductible. We pre-determine and quote your exact out-of-pocket cost.
Per Tooth (Most Common)
Crown additional. Most plans cover 50–80% — billed directly.
Call our emergency line and we'll fit you in today — 7 days a week. Pain relief in one visit is our specialty.

faqs.question: Is it as painful as people say?
faqs.answer: No — and this is the myth we love debunking. The pain you feel before a root canal is from the infection. The procedure itself, with modern anaesthetic, is no more uncomfortable than a filling. Patients are stunned.

faqs.question: How long does it take?
faqs.answer: 60–90 minutes for most teeth. Molars (with multiple canals) may need two visits, about 90 minutes each.

faqs.question: Will I need a crown after?
faqs.answer: Yes — root-canalled teeth become brittle and need a crown to prevent cracking. We typically place this within 2–3 weeks after the root canal heals.

faqs.question: What if I just have it extracted instead?
faqs.answer: Saving your natural tooth is almost always better. Extraction means losing the tooth permanently, then needing a bridge or implant — far more expensive and disruptive.

faqs.question: Is it covered by insurance?
faqs.answer: Yes, most plans cover 50–80% after deductible. We pre-determine and quote your exact out-of-pocket cost.

tooth-extractions.title: Gentle, Calm Tooth Removal — Only When It's Truly Necessary
tooth-extractions.subheading: We do everything to save your natural teeth first. But when extraction is the right call (severe decay, impacted wisdom teeth, or orthodontic prep), we make it comfortable, quick, and stress-free.

paragraphs: Our default is to save the tooth. We'll always explore root canals, crowns, and other options first. But sometimes — a fully impacted wisdom tooth, a fractured root, severe gum disease — extraction is the safest path forward.
We use gentle technique, surgical microscopes for precision, and offer IV sedation for anxious patients or complex cases. Most patients are back to normal in 48–72 hours.
Stop Infection Spreading A severely infected tooth can compromise your jawbone — removal stops it cold.
End Chronic Pain A non-savable tooth can cause endless pain. Extraction is permanent relief.
Comfort-First Technique Modern anaesthetic, IV sedation options, and a gentle hand make it surprisingly easy.
Plan for Replacement We discuss bridges, implants and dentures before extraction so you have a clear path forward.
Confirm extraction is necessary, discuss sedation options, review replacement plans.
Local anaesthetic for all extractions; IV sedation available for impacted teeth or anxious patients.
Modern technique uses gentle rocking — surprisingly little force or pressure required.
Bone graft if planning future implant. Written aftercare and 48-hour follow-up call.
Most teeth can be saved with modern dentistry. But these specific situations often call for extraction — and getting it done right matters.
bullets: Tooth fractured below the gum line
Baby tooth blocking permanent tooth eruption

faqs.question: Does extraction hurt?
faqs.answer: During: no — you're fully numb. After: most patients describe it as 'tender, like a bruise' for 2–3 days, easily managed with ibuprofen. IV sedation makes the experience completely stress-free.

faqs.question: How long is recovery?
faqs.answer: Most patients are back to normal in 48–72 hours. Wisdom teeth take a bit longer — typically 5–7 days. We provide a detailed aftercare plan.

faqs.question: Should I replace the missing tooth?
faqs.answer: If it's a back molar, yes — to prevent neighbouring teeth from shifting. We discuss bridges and implants during the consult so you have a plan before extraction.

faqs.question: Will I need a bone graft?
faqs.answer: If you're planning a future implant, a small bone graft at the extraction site preserves the bone for placement. Most insurance plans cover it.

faqs.question: Is IV sedation safe?
faqs.answer: Yes — administered by our trained anaesthetic team with continuous monitoring. You'll be relaxed throughout, with minimal memory of the procedure.

cosmetic-dentistry.title: Design the Smile You've Always Wanted
cosmetic-dentistry.subheading: From subtle touch-ups to full smile makeovers, our cosmetic team blends artistry with precision engineering — designing smiles that fit your face, age and personality.

paragraphs: Cosmetic dentistry isn't about cookie-cutter Hollywood smiles. Our approach studies your facial features, lip line, age, and goals — then crafts a smile that looks like the best, most confident version of you.
Combining veneers, whitening, bonding, contouring and orthodontics, we offer a full spectrum of options — from a $400 whitening to a $20,000 full makeover.
Tailored to Your Face We design smiles that flatter your specific features — not a one-size-fits-all template.
Try Before You Buy See a digital simulation and even a temporary mockup before any permanent work begins.
Premium Materials We use top-tier porcelain, professional whitening systems, and master ceramists.
Life-Changing Confidence Patients consistently report that a new smile transforms how they speak, laugh, and socialize.
Free 45-minute consult: digital photos, smile simulation, and discussion of your goals and budget.
Our team designs your new smile — you approve every detail before treatment begins.
Whitening, veneers, bonding, or whatever combination — completed comfortably over 1–4 visits.
Final polish, photos, and the smile you've been waiting for. Aftercare plan included.
Whether you have one tooth you'd like fixed or you want a complete smile transformation — there's a cosmetic option (and price point) that fits.
bullets: Wedding, reunion, career change, or just for you

paragraphs: Varies widely — from $2,000 (whitening + bonding) to $20,000+ (full veneers + ortho). The free consult includes a detailed quote so you know exactly what's involved before deciding.
Not the way we do it. Our master ceramist hand-tints each veneer with subtle variation and translucency. Most patients can't tell which teeth are veneers in our portfolio.
Cosmetic-only work is generally not covered. But many smile makeovers include functional work (crowns, fillings) that IS covered. We split the bill so you maximize your benefits.
Veneers: 10–20 years. Whitening: 1–2 years with touch-ups. Bonding: 5–8 years. Crowns: 15–25 years.
Yes — we offer 0% interest financing for 12–24 months on approved credit, no down payment for most patients.
Whitening — Up to $20K for Full Makeovers
Free digital simulation and detailed quote at your consult.
Free digital simulation: walk in, smile for a photo, walk out with a preview of your new smile and a clear written quote.

faqs.question: How much does a full smile makeover cost?
faqs.answer: Varies widely — from $2,000 (whitening + bonding) to $20,000+ (full veneers + ortho). The free consult includes a detailed quote so you know exactly what's involved before deciding.

faqs.question: Will cosmetic work look fake?
faqs.answer: Not the way we do it. Our master ceramist hand-tints each veneer with subtle variation and translucency. Most patients can't tell which teeth are veneers in our portfolio.

faqs.question: Does insurance cover any of it?
faqs.answer: Cosmetic-only work is generally not covered. But many smile makeovers include functional work (crowns, fillings) that IS covered. We split the bill so you maximize your benefits.

faqs.question: How long do results last?
faqs.answer: Veneers: 10–20 years. Whitening: 1–2 years with touch-ups. Bonding: 5–8 years. Crowns: 15–25 years.

faqs.question: Is there a payment plan?
faqs.answer: Yes — we offer 0% interest financing for 12–24 months on approved credit, no down payment for most patients.

porcelain-veneers.title: Hollywood-Caliber Veneers, Hand-Tinted to Look Like You
porcelain-veneers.subheading: Ultra-thin porcelain shells that cover the front of your teeth — fixing chips, gaps, discolouration and shape in just two visits. The fastest cosmetic transformation in dentistry.

paragraphs: Veneers are wafer-thin porcelain facings bonded to the front of your teeth. In just two visits, they can change colour, shape, size, alignment and even fill small gaps — without braces.
Our ceramist hand-tints each veneer with subtle colour variation and translucency. The result: a smile that looks bright, balanced, and unmistakably natural — not fake.
Instant Transformation Walk in with a smile you're unsure about — walk out two visits later with a smile you love.
Stain-Resistant Coffee, wine and tobacco don't stain porcelain like they do natural enamel.
Premium Materials Top-grade porcelain layered by hand — not the budget composite veneers some offices use.
Natural-Looking Subtle variation in shade and translucency makes veneers indistinguishable from natural teeth.
Photos, digital simulation, and detailed discussion of shape, colour and goals.
We remove just 0.5mm of enamel — barely a shave — then take precision digital impressions.
Two weeks at our lab while our ceramist hand-builds each veneer to your exact design.
Veneers are precisely bonded to your teeth. Final polish and photos — and your new smile.
Veneers solve in two visits what would take months with whitening, bonding and orthodontics combined. They're the right call when you want a complete transformation, fast.
bullets: Chipped, cracked or worn front teeth

paragraphs: No — modern veneers require removing only 0.5mm of enamel (about the thickness of a fingernail). The underlying tooth structure is preserved. With proper care, your teeth stay healthy under the veneers for decades.
Not when designed properly. We use multiple shades, varied translucency, and slight asymmetry — exactly how natural teeth look. The 'fake' veneer look comes from over-uniform colour and shape.
10–20 years with proper care. Avoid using your front teeth to bite ice or hard objects, wear a nightguard if you grind, and maintain regular cleanings.
Individual veneers can be replaced. Issues are rare with quality bonding and proper care, but we warranty all veneers for 5 years against material defects.
$1,200–$1,800 per tooth depending on the case. A typical 8-veneer smile makeover runs $10,000–$14,000. Financing available.
Per Hand-Crafted Veneer
0% financing available. Free smile simulation at consult.
Free digital smile simulation. We'll show you exactly what your new smile could look like — no commitment to move forward.

faqs.question: Do veneers ruin your real teeth?
faqs.answer: No — modern veneers require removing only 0.5mm of enamel (about the thickness of a fingernail). The underlying tooth structure is preserved. With proper care, your teeth stay healthy under the veneers for decades.

faqs.question: Will they look fake or 'too white'?
faqs.answer: Not when designed properly. We use multiple shades, varied translucency, and slight asymmetry — exactly how natural teeth look. The 'fake' veneer look comes from over-uniform colour and shape.

faqs.question: How long do they last?
faqs.answer: 10–20 years with proper care. Avoid using your front teeth to bite ice or hard objects, wear a nightguard if you grind, and maintain regular cleanings.

faqs.question: What if one breaks?
faqs.answer: Individual veneers can be replaced. Issues are rare with quality bonding and proper care, but we warranty all veneers for 5 years against material defects.

faqs.question: How much do they cost?
faqs.answer: $1,200–$1,800 per tooth depending on the case. A typical 8-veneer smile makeover runs $10,000–$14,000. Financing available.
